name: विभूतियोग
teaching: Wherever you find splendour — in rivers the Ganga, in mountains Meru, in speech the single syllable, in the silent the silence — you are seeing a spark of one fire. Krishna lists his glories not as autobiography but as a training of the eye: learn to trace every excellence back to its source. The whole universe stands on a fragment of it. Every splendor in the world is a fingerprint: the catalog of glories trains the eye until one rule suffices — whatever shines anywhere is a spark of one fire. He stands at the heart of all beings, and one fragment of Him sustains everything.
questions: What most recently stopped you with its beauty or power — and did you follow it back to its source?
Among the glories Krishna names are gambling of the dishonest and the rod of punishers. Can you find the sacred even in what disturbs you?
If you listed your own ten glories, what would the list reveal about what you worship?

verses.devanagari:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
भूय एव महाबाहो शृणु मे परमं वचः ।
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया॥
verses.transliteration:
bhūya eva mahābāho śṛṇu me paramaṃ vacaḥ
yatte'haṃ prīyamāṇāya vakṣyāmi hitakāmyayā
verses.essence: Again, mighty-armed one, hear my supreme word — which I speak to you, who delight in it, desiring your good.
verses.translation: Again, O mighty-armed Arjuna, listen to my supreme word which I will declare to thee who who art beloved, for thy welfare.

anvaya.meaning: yet again, O mighty-armed
hear my supreme word
which I, to you who are delighting
shall speak, desiring your welfare

verses.explanation: Two reasons the teaching continues past its own summaries: prīyamāṇāya — Arjuna is *enjoying* this, drinking it in — and hita-kāmyayā, the speaker wants his good. Revelation here is not a syllabus to complete but a generosity that keeps overflowing wherever it finds delight.
verses.connection: Reopens the discourse after chapter 9's seal — love of the listener as sufficient cause.
verses.reflection: Stay for the encore today: when you think you have heard it all, love says 'further — listen.'
verses.background: Krishna, unprompted now, continues for the joy of the listener: hear again My supreme word, spoken for your good because you are beloved. Neither gods nor great sages know My origin, for I am their origin; the wise who know Me as unborn Lord are freed — every quality of beings, from intellect to fear and fearlessness, arises from Me alone.

verses.devanagari:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः ।
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः॥
verses.transliteration:
na me viduḥ suragaṇāḥ prabhavaṃ na maharṣayaḥ
ahamādirhi devānāṃ maharṣīṇāṃ ca sarvaśaḥ
verses.essence: Neither the hosts of gods nor the great sages know my origin — for I am, in every way, the source of the gods and the great sages.
verses.translation: Neither the hosts of the gods nor the great sages know My origin; for in every way I am the source of all the gods and the great sages.

anvaya.meaning: the hosts of gods do not know
my origin, nor the great sages
for I am the beginning of the gods
and of the great sages, in every way

verses.explanation: The epistemological ceiling: even the highest created knowers cannot see behind their own beginning — the lamp cannot stand behind its own light. Whatever gods and seers report of him is testimony from downstream; the source remains upstream of every witness.
verses.connection: Sets the limit that makes 10.3's knower so rare: God's origin is structurally unwitnessable.
verses.reflection: Respect the mystery's depth: even the gods cannot trace His origin. Let your not-knowing relax.
verses.background: Krishna, unprompted now, continues for the joy of the listener: hear again My supreme word, spoken for your good because you are beloved. Neither gods nor great sages know My origin, for I am their origin; the wise who know Me as unborn Lord are freed — every quality of beings, from intellect to fear and fearlessness, arises from Me alone.

verses.devanagari:
यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम् ।
असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥
verses.transliteration:
yo māmajamanādiṃ ca vetti lokamaheśvaram
asammūḍhaḥ sa martyeṣu sarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate
verses.essence: One who knows me as unborn, beginningless, the great Lord of the worlds — that one, undeluded among mortals, is released from all sins.
verses.translation: He who knows Me as unborn and beginningless, as the great Lord of the worlds, he, among mortals, is undeluded and he is liberated from all sins.

anvaya.meaning: who me, unborn and beginningless
knows as the great Lord of the worlds
that one, undeluded among mortals
is released from all sins

verses.explanation: What cannot be known by inspection (10.2) can be known by recognition: ajam, anādim, the one who never began. The verse's strange economics returns from 4.36: this single accurate recognition unbinds *all* sins — because sin's root is misplacing the center of the world, and this knowledge places it.
verses.connection: The human counterpart to 10.2's divine unknowability: right relation substitutes for impossible inspection.
verses.reflection: Know one thing greatly today — the unborn, beginningless Lord — and watch lesser confusions release.
verses.background: Krishna, unprompted now, continues for the joy of the listener: hear again My supreme word, spoken for your good because you are beloved. Neither gods nor great sages know My origin, for I am their origin; the wise who know Me as unborn Lord are freed — every quality of beings, from intellect to fear and fearlessness, arises from Me alone.

verses.devanagari:
बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसम्मोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः ।
सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च॥
verses.transliteration:
buddhirjñānamasammohaḥ kṣamā satyaṃ damaḥ śamaḥ
sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ bhavo'bhāvo bhayaṃ cābhayameva ca
verses.essence: Understanding, knowledge, freedom from delusion, patience, truth, restraint, calm; pleasure and pain, becoming and ceasing, fear and fearlessness —
verses.translation: Intellect, wisdom, non-delusion, forgiveness, truth, self-restraint, calmness, happiness, pain, existence or birth, non-existence or death, fear and also fearlessness.

anvaya.meaning: understanding, knowledge, non-delusion
patience, truth, restraint, tranquility
pleasure, pain, becoming, ceasing
and fear, and fearlessness

verses.explanation: An inventory of the inner weather begins — and it deliberately mixes columns: wisdom beside pain, truth beside fear. Whatever a human being can be or feel, the list is gathering it all toward one source. No state of mind, it will claim, is self-made.
verses.connection: First half of the human-condition catalogue completed in 10.5.
verses.reflection: Notice today's inner weather — clarity, patience, truth, even fear — and read the watermark: from Him.
verses.background: Krishna, unprompted now, continues for the joy of the listener: hear again My supreme word, spoken for your good because you are beloved. Neither gods nor great sages know My origin, for I am their origin; the wise who know Me as unborn Lord are freed — every quality of beings, from intellect to fear and fearlessness, arises from Me alone.

verses.devanagari:
अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः ।
भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः॥
verses.transliteration:
ahiṃsā samatā tuṣṭistapo dānaṃ yaśo'yaśaḥ
bhavanti bhāvā bhūtānāṃ matta eva pṛthagvidhāḥ
verses.essence: — non-injury, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, fame and infamy: these manifold states of beings arise from me alone.
verses.translation: Non-injury, eanimity, contentment, austerity, beneficence, fame, ill-fame (these) different kinds of alities of beings arise from Me alone.

anvaya.meaning: non-injury, equanimity, contentment
austerity, charity, fame, infamy
arise the states of beings
from me alone, of various kinds

verses.explanation: The catalogue closes on its point: matta eva, from me alone — courage and cowardice, honor and disgrace, all drawn from one well at different depths. Nothing in the psyche is foreign territory to him; even the states we are ashamed of are made of borrowed being.
verses.connection: Seals 10.4–5: the whole interior life sourced — preparing the outer-world catalogue to come.
verses.reflection: Trace one quality you prize in yourself to its source, and hold it more lightly, more gratefully.
verses.background: Krishna, unprompted now, continues for the joy of the listener: hear again My supreme word, spoken for your good because you are beloved. Neither gods nor great sages know My origin, for I am their origin; the wise who know Me as unborn Lord are freed — every quality of beings, from intellect to fear and fearlessness, arises from Me alone.

verses.devanagari:
महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा ।
मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः॥
verses.transliteration:
maharṣayaḥ sapta pūrve catvāro manavastathā
madbhāvā mānasā jātā yeṣāṃ loka imāḥ prajāḥ
verses.essence: The seven great sages of old, and the four, and the Manus — beings of my nature, born of my mind — from whom all these creatures in the world descend.
verses.translation: The seven great sages, the ancient four and also the Manus, possessed of powers like Me (on account of their minds being fixed on Me), were born of (My) mind; from them are these creatures born in this world.

anvaya.meaning: the seven great sages of old
the four, and likewise the Manus
of my being, born of my mind
from whom, in the world, these creatures

verses.explanation: Genealogy compressed: the seven seers, the four primordial ones, the Manus — humanity's traditional founding fathers — were themselves mānasāḥ, thought into being, mind-born of him. Trace any family tree far enough and it roots not in soil but in a meditation.
verses.connection: Extends the sourcing from inner states (10.4–5) to the human lineage itself.
verses.reflection: Honor the ancient lineage you stand in — every teacher's teacher traces to one mind.
verses.background: Krishna, unprompted now, continues for the joy of the listener: hear again My supreme word, spoken for your good because you are beloved. Neither gods nor great sages know My origin, for I am their origin; the wise who know Me as unborn Lord are freed — every quality of beings, from intellect to fear and fearlessness, arises from Me alone.

verses.devanagari:
एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ।
सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः॥
verses.transliteration:
etāṃ vibhūtiṃ yogaṃ ca mama yo vetti tattvataḥ
so'vikampena yogena yujyate nātra saṃśayaḥ
verses.essence: One who knows in truth this pervading power and yoga of mine is harnessed in unshakable yoga — of this there is no doubt.
verses.translation: He who in truth knows these manifold manifestations of My Being and (this) Yoga-power of Mine becomes established in the unshakable Yoga; there is no doubt about it.

anvaya.meaning: this vibhuti and yoga
of mine, who knows in truth
that one, with unwavering yoga
is yoked — herein no doubt

verses.explanation: The chapter's keyword arrives: vibhūti — the pervading manifestation, the glory-in-things. Knowing it tattvataḥ produces avikampa yoga, a union that no longer trembles: a vision of God braided through everything cannot be dislodged by any one thing going wrong.
verses.connection: Names the chapter's subject and its fruit: steadiness purchased by seeing him everywhere.
verses.reflection: Let conviction in this glory steady you once today — unwavering yoga begins as settled knowing.
verses.background: Krishna, unprompted now, continues for the joy of the listener: hear again My supreme word, spoken for your good because you are beloved. Neither gods nor great sages know My origin, for I am their origin; the wise who know Me as unborn Lord are freed — every quality of beings, from intellect to fear and fearlessness, arises from Me alone.

verses.devanagari:
अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते ।
इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः॥
verses.transliteration:
ahaṃ sarvasya prabhavo mattaḥ sarvaṃ pravartate
iti matvā bhajante māṃ budhā bhāvasamanvitāḥ
verses.essence: I am the source of all; from me everything proceeds. Realizing this, the wise worship me, filled with loving feeling.
verses.translation: I am the source of all; from Me everything evolves; understanding thus, the wise, endowed with meditation, worship Me.

anvaya.meaning: I am the origin of all
from me everything proceeds
thus realizing, they worship me
the wise, endowed with deep feeling

verses.explanation: First of four verses the tradition treats as the Gita's distilled heart. The metaphysics is one line; the second line shows what metaphysics is *for*: iti matvā — having truly absorbed it — the wise do not merely conclude; they worship, bhāva-samanvitāḥ, flooded with feeling. Understood origin becomes overflowing love; thought completes itself as devotion. Aham sarvasya prabhavo — I am the source of all; from Me everything proceeds: the first of the four verses (10.8-11) the Gaudiya tradition isolates as the chatuh-shloki, the Gita's seed within the Gita. Iti matva bhajante mam — knowing this, the wise worship: the commentaries stress the order — metaphysics ripening into adoration, budha bhava-samanvitah, the awakened, endowed with feeling.
verses.connection: Opens the chatuh-shloki (10.8–11), the four verses many call the whole Gita in miniature.
verses.schools_note: For the Gaudiya school, 10.8-11 is the chatuh-shloki — the Gita's four seed-verses, its whole theology in miniature: He the source (8), the satsang of lovers (9), the gift of buddhi-yoga (10), the inner lamp (11). Shankara reads the same span as knowledge culminating teaching. The shared observation: here doctrine, community, grace, and illumination appear in one unbroken breath.
verses.reflection: Say the seed-verse once with feeling: He is the origin of all; from Him everything proceeds.
verses.background: Four verses the tradition treasures as the Gita's seed: I am the source of all, from Me all proceeds — the wise worship Me with feeling; their minds and lives in Me, they delight in speaking of Me; to these ever-yoked lovers I give the yoga of understanding; dwelling in their own hearts, I destroy their darkness with the lamp of knowledge.

verses.devanagari:
मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम् ।
कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च॥
verses.transliteration:
maccittā madgataprāṇā bodhayantaḥ parasparam
kathayantaśca māṃ nityaṃ tuṣyanti ca ramanti ca
verses.essence: With minds in me, with lives surrendered to me, enlightening one another and speaking of me always — they are content, and they rejoice.
verses.translation: With their mind and their life wholly absorbed in Me, enlightening each other and ever speaking of Me, they are satisfied and delighted.

anvaya.meaning: their minds in me, their life-breath gone to me
enlightening one another
and speaking of me constantly
they are content, and they rejoice

verses.explanation: The lovers' commune: hearts and very breath relocated into him, and then — the verse's warmth — bodhayantaḥ parasparam, teaching *each other*, no hierarchy, the talk of him passing around like bread. The last four syllables are the proof: tuṣyanti ca ramanti ca — this life satisfies *and* delights. Devotion's secret is that it is enjoyable. Mach-chitta mad-gata-prana — minds in Me, lives gone into Me, enlightening one another, speaking of Me always: the satsang verse, the scriptural warrant for every gathering that ever sat in a circle to talk of God. Tushyanti cha ramanti cha — they are satisfied and they delight: the tradition reads the two verbs as contentment and ecstasy, the twin wages of holy company.
verses.connection: The community portrait inside the four core verses: shared remembrance as continuous feast.
verses.reflection: Spend ten minutes in the satsang of 10.9: thinking of Him, speaking of Him — and notice the contentment.
verses.background: Four verses the tradition treasures as the Gita's seed: I am the source of all, from Me all proceeds — the wise worship Me with feeling; their minds and lives in Me, they delight in speaking of Me; to these ever-yoked lovers I give the yoga of understanding; dwelling in their own hearts, I destroy their darkness with the lamp of knowledge.

verses.devanagari:
तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम् ।
ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते॥
verses.transliteration:
teṣāṃ satatayuktānāṃ bhajatāṃ prītipūrvakam
dadāmi buddhiyogaṃ taṃ yena māmupayānti te
verses.essence: To these, ever harnessed, worshiping with love, I give the yoga of understanding by which they come to me.
verses.translation: To them who are ever steadfast, worshipping Me with love, I give the Yoga of discrimination by which they come to Me.

anvaya.meaning: to them, the constantly yoked
worshiping with love
I give that buddhi-yoga
by which they come near to me

verses.explanation: The direction of the decisive gift reverses: dadāmi — *I give*. The understanding that finally carries one home is not achieved at the summit of effort; it is bestowed midway into love. Prīti-pūrvakam is the trigger: where worship has become affection, the inner teacher takes over the curriculum. Tesham satata-yuktanam — to those constantly yoked, worshiping with love, dadami buddhi-yogam tam: I give the yoga of understanding by which they come to Me. The commentaries mark the grammar of grace: dadami, I give — the decisive insight is bestowed, not built. Priti-purvakam, lovingly: the inner teacher's tuition, the schools agree, is paid in affection.
verses.connection: The grace-verse of the four: human love ascends, divine intelligence descends to meet it.
verses.schools_note: A cornerstone for the bhakti schools: the final knowledge is gift, not conquest — prasada crowning prema. Shankara too reads it as the Lord granting the very buddhi by which liberation dawns; the disagreement is only over how much the receiver's hands were ever empty.
verses.reflection: Practice with love today and expect the inside gift: the buddhi-yoga by which one comes to Him.
verses.background: Four verses the tradition treasures as the Gita's seed: I am the source of all, from Me all proceeds — the wise worship Me with feeling; their minds and lives in Me, they delight in speaking of Me; to these ever-yoked lovers I give the yoga of understanding; dwelling in their own hearts, I destroy their darkness with the lamp of knowledge.

verses.devanagari:
तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः ।
नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता॥
verses.transliteration:
teṣāmevānukampārthamahamajñānajaṃ tamaḥ
nāśayāmyātmabhāvastho jñānadīpena bhāsvatā
verses.essence: Out of compassion for them, dwelling in their own hearts, I destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the shining lamp of knowledge.
verses.translation: Out of mere compassion for them, I, dwelling within their Self, destroy the darkness born of ignorance by the luminous lamp of knowledge.

anvaya.meaning: for the sake of compassion toward them
I, the ignorance-born darkness
destroy — dwelling in their own being
with the radiant lamp of knowledge

verses.explanation: Where the gift of 10.10 is delivered from: ātma-bhāva-sthaḥ — stationed within their own selfhood, not shouting from a heaven. Anukampā: compassion, literally a trembling-along-with. The image completes the chapter's interior turn: the divine does not hand the devotee a lamp; he *becomes* the lamp, lit from inside the very room that was dark. Tesham evanukampartham — out of sheer compassion for them, I, dwelling in their own self, destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the shining lamp of knowledge. Atma-bhava-sthah: the lamp is lit from inside the house. Commentators call this the Gita's portrait of the antaryami at work — grace not as intervention from above but illumination from the room's own center.
verses.connection: Closes the four core verses: the whole drama of liberation relocated to within the lover's own heart.
verses.schools_note: 'Nashayamy atma-bhava-sthah' — dwelling in their own being, I destroy their darkness: the classical proof-text for the antaryami's grace. The schools differ on mechanism — Advaita: the inner Self's light dispelling superimposition; Vishishtadvaita: the Lord personally tending the lamp within His devotee — but agree on the location: liberation is lit from inside the house, never imported.
verses.reflection: Ask once for the lamp, not the map — the indwelling light that destroys darkness at its station.
verses.background: Four verses the tradition treasures as the Gita's seed: I am the source of all, from Me all proceeds — the wise worship Me with feeling; their minds and lives in Me, they delight in speaking of Me; to these ever-yoked lovers I give the yoga of understanding; dwelling in their own hearts, I destroy their darkness with the lamp of knowledge.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन उवाच
verses.devanagari:
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान् ।
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम्॥
verses.transliteration:
paraṃ brahma paraṃ dhāma pavitraṃ paramaṃ bhavān
puruṣaṃ śāśvataṃ divyamādidevamajaṃ vibhum
verses.essence: Arjuna says: You are the supreme Brahman, the supreme abode, the supreme purifier — the eternal divine Purusha, the primal god, unborn, all-pervading.
verses.translation: Thou art the Supreme Brahman, the supreme abode (or the supreme light), the supreme purifier, eternal, divine Person, the primeval God, unborn andn omnipresent.

anvaya.meaning: the supreme Brahman, the supreme abode
the supreme purifier are you
the eternal divine Purusha
the primal god, unborn, all-pervading

verses.explanation: Arjuna can hold silence no longer; the four verses have lit him, and his answer is a cascade of the tradition's highest titles, poured out in one breath. Notice the movement: the teaching of 10.8–11 was received not as information but as presence, and presence demands address, not notes.
verses.connection: Arjuna's response to the chatuh-shloki: theology turning, mid-sentence, into praise.
verses.reflection: Praise before petitioning today: name the highest as highest, and feel your own scale correct itself.
verses.background: Arjuna responds — the first time in the poem he confesses full faith: You are the supreme Brahman, declared so by Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa, and now by Yourself; neither gods nor demons know Your manifestation. Then the request that shapes the chapter: tell me Your divine glories, by which You pervade the worlds — for I am never sated of hearing.

verses.devanagari:
आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा ।
असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे॥
verses.transliteration:
āhustvāmṛṣayaḥ sarve devarṣirnāradastathā
asito devalo vyāsaḥ svayaṃ caiva bravīṣi me
verses.essence: So declare all the sages — the divine sage Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa — and you yourself declare it to me.
verses.translation: All the sages have thus declared Thee, as also the divine sage Narada; so also Asita, Devala and Vyasa; and now Thou Thyself sayest so to me.

anvaya.meaning: all the sages declare you (thus)
the divine sage Narada likewise
Asita, Devala, Vyasa
and you yourself tell me

verses.explanation: Arjuna cites his sources like a careful witness: the consensus of the seers, the most venerated names — and then the testimony that outranks them all: svayaṁ caiva bravīṣi me, you yourself are telling me. Tradition corroborates; the living voice confirms. Faith, here, is not a leap but a convergence.
verses.connection: Grounds 10.12's outburst: revelation cross-checked against the whole lineage of seers.
verses.reflection: Borrow the seers' testimony when yours wavers — Narada, Vyasa, and the chain of those who saw.
verses.background: Arjuna responds — the first time in the poem he confesses full faith: You are the supreme Brahman, declared so by Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa, and now by Yourself; neither gods nor demons know Your manifestation. Then the request that shapes the chapter: tell me Your divine glories, by which You pervade the worlds — for I am never sated of hearing.

verses.devanagari:
सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव ।
न हि ते भगवन्व्यक्तिं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः॥
verses.transliteration:
sarvametadṛtaṃ manye yanmāṃ vadasi keśava
na hi te bhagavanvyaktiṃ vidurdevā na dānavāḥ
verses.essence: All this that you tell me, Keshava, I hold to be true. Indeed, Lord, neither the gods nor the danavas know your manifestation.
verses.translation: I believe all this that Thou sayest to me to be true, O Krishna; verily, O blessed Lord! neither the gods nor the demons know Thy manifestation (origin).

anvaya.meaning: all this I hold to be true (rita)
which you tell me, O Keshava
for not, O Lord, your manifestation
do the gods know, nor the danavas

verses.explanation: The word Arjuna chooses is ancient and exact: ṛtam — not merely accurate but true the way cosmic order is true. And he has absorbed 10.2's lesson: if neither gods nor their rivals can see behind the source, then the source's own self-disclosure is the only possible window — and he is standing at it.
verses.connection: Arjuna's act of faith stated formally — acceptance grounded in 10.2's logic of testimony.
verses.reflection: Accept one truth today on the speaker's authority, where neither gods nor demons could verify it.
verses.background: Arjuna responds — the first time in the poem he confesses full faith: You are the supreme Brahman, declared so by Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa, and now by Yourself; neither gods nor demons know Your manifestation. Then the request that shapes the chapter: tell me Your divine glories, by which You pervade the worlds — for I am never sated of hearing.

verses.devanagari:
स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम ।
भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते॥
verses.transliteration:
svayamevātmanātmānaṃ vettha tvaṃ puruṣottama
bhūtabhāvana bhūteśa devadeva jagatpate
verses.essence: You alone know yourself by yourself, Purushottama — source of beings, Lord of beings, God of gods, Master of the universe.
verses.translation: Swami Sivananda did not comment on this sloka

anvaya.meaning: yourself alone, by yourself, yourself
you know, O Purushottama
O source of beings, Lord of beings
God of gods, Master of the world

verses.explanation: The epistemology completed: svayam evātmanātmānam — self knowing self by self, the one knowledge with no outside vantage and no instrument but its own light. Hence the request that follows is the only rational move left: if you alone hold the map, unroll it for me.
verses.connection: Sets up 10.16's petition: since self-knowledge of God is God's monopoly, disclosure must be asked for.
verses.reflection: Rest in this: You alone know Yourself by Yourself. Stand at the edge of that and bow.
verses.background: Arjuna responds — the first time in the poem he confesses full faith: You are the supreme Brahman, declared so by Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa, and now by Yourself; neither gods nor demons know Your manifestation. Then the request that shapes the chapter: tell me Your divine glories, by which You pervade the worlds — for I am never sated of hearing.

verses.devanagari:
वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः ।
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि॥
verses.transliteration:
vaktumarhasyaśeṣeṇa divyā hyātmavibhūtayaḥ
yābhirvibhūtibhirlokānimāṃstvaṃ vyāpya tiṣṭhasi
verses.essence: Please describe without remainder your divine self-manifestations — the pervading powers by which you abide, filling all these worlds.
verses.translation: Thou shouldst indeed tell, without reserve, of Thy divine glories by which Thou existest, pervading all these worlds. (None else can do so.)

anvaya.meaning: you should declare without remainder
for divine are the self-manifestations
by which vibhutis, the worlds
these, you stand pervading

verses.explanation: The formal request that generates the rest of the chapter: tell me your vibhūtis — where, in this teeming world, the divine current runs closest to the surface. Arjuna is asking for handholds: the infinite is unclimbable; its outcroppings are not.
verses.connection: Commissions the great catalogue of 10.20–39.
verses.reflection: Ask sincerely to be shown the divine glories in your own ordinary rooms today.
verses.background: Arjuna responds — the first time in the poem he confesses full faith: You are the supreme Brahman, declared so by Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa, and now by Yourself; neither gods nor demons know Your manifestation. Then the request that shapes the chapter: tell me Your divine glories, by which You pervade the worlds — for I am never sated of hearing.

verses.devanagari:
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन् ।
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया॥
verses.transliteration:
kathaṃ vidyāmahaṃ yogiṃstvāṃ sadā paricintayan
keṣu keṣu ca bhāveṣu cintyo'si bhagavanmayā
verses.essence: How may I know you, O Yogi, by constant meditation? And in what various states of being are you to be contemplated by me, Lord?
verses.translation: How shall I, ever meditating, know Thee, O Yogin? In what aspects or things, O blessed Lord, art Thou to be thought of by me?

anvaya.meaning: how may I know you, O Yogin
you, ever contemplating
and in which, which states
are you to be thought of by me, O Lord

verses.explanation: The practical heart of the request: keṣu keṣu bhāveṣu — in which things, exactly? Arjuna wants a meditator's field guide: not a doctrine of omnipresence but a list of places where omnipresence shows. The mind cannot grip 'everywhere'; it can grip the sun, the lion, the Ganga.
verses.connection: Refines 10.16 into the chapter's true genre: a contemplative's gazetteer.
verses.reflection: Pick your forms for meditating today — Arjuna's question is yours: in what aspects shall I think of You?
verses.background: Arjuna responds — the first time in the poem he confesses full faith: You are the supreme Brahman, declared so by Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa, and now by Yourself; neither gods nor demons know Your manifestation. Then the request that shapes the chapter: tell me Your divine glories, by which You pervade the worlds — for I am never sated of hearing.

verses.devanagari:
विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन ।
भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि शृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम्॥
verses.transliteration:
vistareṇātmano yogaṃ vibhūtiṃ ca janārdana
bhūyaḥ kathaya tṛptirhi śṛṇvato nāsti me'mṛtam
verses.essence: Tell me again, in full, your yoga and your pervading glory, Janardana — for I am never sated of hearing this nectar.
verses.translation: Tell me again in detail, O Krishna, of Thy Yogic power and glory; for I am not satiated with what I have heard of Thy life-giving and nectar-like speech.

anvaya.meaning: in detail, your own yoga
and vibhuti, O Janardana
tell again — for satiety
of hearing the nectar, there is none for me

verses.explanation: The listener's confession that licenses the longest answer in the chapter: amṛtam — this is nectar, and nectar has no fill-line. Tṛptir nāsti: the appetite that grows by feeding, the one hunger the Gita everywhere approves. Teaching flows where thirst like this waits.
verses.connection: Completes the petition (10.12–18); the floodgates of the catalogue open next.
verses.reflection: Hear the nectar past fullness once: ask for more of what genuinely nourishes, unashamed.
verses.background: Arjuna responds — the first time in the poem he confesses full faith: You are the supreme Brahman, declared so by Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa, and now by Yourself; neither gods nor demons know Your manifestation. Then the request that shapes the chapter: tell me Your divine glories, by which You pervade the worlds — for I am never sated of hearing.

verses.devanagari:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः ।
प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे॥
verses.transliteration:
hanta te kathayiṣyāmi divyā hyātmavibhūtayaḥ
prādhānyataḥ kuruśreṣṭha nāstyanto vistarasya me
verses.essence: Yes — I shall recount to you my divine self-manifestations, but only the chief of them, best of Kurus; for there is no end to my extent.
verses.translation: Very well! Now I will declare to thee My divine glories in their prominence, O Arjuna; there is no end to their detailed description.

anvaya.meaning: ah yes — I shall recount to you
the divine self-manifestations
by way of the principal ones, O best of Kurus
there is no end to my extent

verses.explanation: Hanta — the warm exclamation of a teacher delighted to be asked. Then the honest disclaimer that frames everything to come: prādhānyataḥ, only headline entries; the full inventory has no last page. The catalogue is a sampler, not a census — finite windows cut into an infinite wall.
verses.connection: Accepts 10.16–18's commission with its terms: highlights only, for the whole is endless.
verses.reflection: Accept the summary nature of all teaching — the detail is endless; let the chief glories suffice today.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः ।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च॥
verses.transliteration:
ahamātmā guḍākeśa sarvabhūtāśayasthitaḥ
ahamādiśca madhyaṃ ca bhūtānāmanta eva ca
verses.essence: I am the Self, Gudakesha, seated in the heart of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and the very end of beings.
verses.translation: I am the Self, O Gudakesa, seated in the hearts of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle and also the end of all beings.

anvaya.meaning: I am the Self, O Gudakesha
seated in the heart of all beings
I am the beginning and the middle
and the very end of beings

verses.explanation: The catalogue's first entry is deliberately its deepest, placed first so nothing after it can be misread: before sun or lion or Ganga, I am the ātmā in every heart — including the one now listening. Whoever forgets the rest of the list but holds this line has the whole chapter; every later glory is this nearness, externalized. Aham atma gudakesha sarva-bhutashaya-sthitah — I am the Self seated in the heart of all beings: the vibhuti list's first entry, given before sun or scripture, and the commentaries insist on the order: find Him first as the innermost, or the catalog becomes idolatry. Aham adish cha madhyam cha — beginning, middle, end of beings: the frame within which every following glory is a footnote.
verses.connection: The master-key issued before the catalogue: all outer vibhutis are footnotes to the indwelling one.
verses.schools_note: Commentators treat this as the chapter's governing verse: Shankara reads it as the non-dual Self announced plainly; Ramanuja as the inner controller (antaryami) dwelling in every soul. Both agree the list that follows must be read through it — glories outside as pointers to the glory within.
verses.reflection: Practice the first vibhuti everywhere: greet the Self seated in the heart of each being you meet.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान् ।
मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी॥
verses.transliteration:
ādityānāmahaṃ viṣṇurjyotiṣāṃ raviraṃśumān
marīcirmarutāmasmi nakṣatrāṇāmahaṃ śaśī
verses.essence: Of the Adityas I am Vishnu; of lights, the radiant sun; of the Maruts I am Marichi; among the stars I am the moon.
verses.translation: Among the (twelve) Adityas, I am Vishnu; among luminaries, the radiant sun; I am Marichi among the (seven or forty-nine) Maruts; among stars the moon am I.

anvaya.meaning: of the Adityas I am Vishnu
of lights, the ray-crowned sun
of the Maruts I am Marichi
of the lunar mansions, the moon

verses.explanation: The pattern is set: in every class, the member where the class's essence burns brightest. Among lights, the sun — not because the rest are dark, but because there the principle of light is least veiled. The list teaches a way of seeing: find the summit of anything, and you have found a window.
verses.connection: First entry of the formula that runs to 10.38: 'of each kind, its crown.'
verses.reflection: Find Him today as the brightest of each class you encounter — the sun among lights, to begin.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः ।
इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना॥
verses.transliteration:
vedānāṃ sāmavedo'smi devānāmasmi vāsavaḥ
indriyāṇāṃ manaścāsmi bhūtānāmasmi cetanā
verses.essence: Of the Vedas I am the Sama Veda; of the gods I am Indra; of the senses I am the mind; in beings I am consciousness.
verses.translation: Among the Vedas I am the Sama-Veda; I am Vasava among the gods; among the senses I am the mind; and I am intelligence among living beings.

anvaya.meaning: of the Vedas I am the Sama
of the gods I am Vasava (Indra)
of the senses I am the mind
of beings I am consciousness

verses.explanation: Of the Vedas, the one that is sung — scripture at its most musical. Then inward: among the senses, the mind that coordinates them; and in all creatures, cetanā, sheer sentience itself. The catalogue keeps alternating outward and inward, refusing to let the seeker look in only one direction.
verses.connection: The formula turns interior: the divine located in awareness itself.
verses.reflection: Among your senses, find Him as the mind; among your hymns, as the one you actually sing.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम् ।
वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
rudrāṇāṃ śaṅkaraścāsmi vitteśo yakṣarakṣasām
vasūnāṃ pāvakaścāsmi meruḥ śikhariṇāmaham
verses.essence: Of the Rudras I am Shankara; of yakshas and rakshasas, the lord of wealth; of the Vasus I am fire; of mountain peaks I am Meru.
verses.translation: And, among the Rudras I am Sankara; among the Yakshas and Rakshasas, the Lord of wealth (Kubera); among the Vasus I am Pavaka (fire); and among the (seven) mountains I am the Meru.

anvaya.meaning: of the Rudras I am Shankara
the lord of wealth (Kubera), of yakshas and rakshasas
of the Vasus I am fire
of peaked mountains, Meru

verses.explanation: Shankara — the auspicious one, Shiva himself — named as his own vibhūti: the catalogue quietly absorbs even the other great God of the tradition into its embrace. The peaks of every pantheon and landscape are claimed, not as rivals annexed but as facets owned.
verses.connection: The catalogue at its most ecumenical: the divine names of others, gathered in.
verses.reflection: Spot the summit of each small mountain range in your day, and name the summit His.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम् ।
सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः॥
verses.transliteration:
purodhasāṃ ca mukhyaṃ māṃ viddhi pārtha bṛhaspatim
senānīnāmahaṃ skandaḥ sarasāmasmi sāgaraḥ
verses.essence: Of priests, Partha, know me as the chief, Brihaspati; of commanders I am Skanda; of waters I am the ocean.
verses.translation: And, among the household priests (of kings), O Arjuna, know Me to be the chief, Brihaspati; among the army generals I am Skana; among lakes I am the ocean. '

anvaya.meaning: and of household priests, the chief, me
know, O Partha, as Brihaspati
of army-leaders I am Skanda
of waters I am the ocean

verses.explanation: Three vocations, three crowns: the priesthood's wisest voice, the general the gods themselves chose, and — among all gathered waters — the sea, into which every river resigns its name. Each is the point where a whole category becomes unmistakable.
verses.connection: Continues the survey through office and element alike.
verses.reflection: Of your inner generals and priests, ask which is Skanda, which Brihaspati — He claims the chiefs.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम् ।
यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः॥
verses.transliteration:
maharṣīṇāṃ bhṛgurahaṃ girāmasmyekamakṣaram
yajñānāṃ japayajño'smi sthāvarāṇāṃ himālayaḥ
verses.essence: Of the great sages I am Bhrigu; of utterances, the single syllable Om; of sacrifices I am the sacrifice of silent repetition; of immovable things, the Himalaya.
verses.translation: Among the great sages I am Bhrigu; among words I am the one syllable (Om); among sacrifices I am the sacrifice of silent repetition; among the immovable things I am the Himalayas.

anvaya.meaning: of the great sages I am Bhrigu
of utterances, the one syllable (Om)
of sacrifices I am the japa-sacrifice
of immovables, the Himalaya

verses.explanation: Among all sacrifices, the quietest: japa, the whispered or silent repetition that needs no fire, fee, or priest — ranked here above every grand altar. The verse pairs it with Om, speech reduced to seed, and the Himalaya, mass become stillness: three forms of the same monumental quiet.
verses.connection: A verse the contemplative traditions treasured: interiority crowned over spectacle.
verses.reflection: Of all your utterances today, let one be the single syllable; of your offerings, the silent repetition.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः ।
गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः॥
verses.transliteration:
aśvatthaḥ sarvavṛkṣāṇāṃ devarṣīṇāṃ ca nāradaḥ
gandharvāṇāṃ citrarathaḥ siddhānāṃ kapilo muniḥ
verses.essence: Of all trees I am the ashvattha; of the divine sages, Narada; of the gandharvas, Chitraratha; of the perfected ones, the sage Kapila.
verses.translation: Among all the trees ( I am) the Peepul; among the divine sages, I am Narada; among Gandharvas, Chitraratha; among the perfected, the sage Kapila.

anvaya.meaning: of all trees, the ashvattha
and of the divine sages, Narada
of the gandharvas, Chitraratha
of the perfected, the sage Kapila

verses.explanation: The sacred fig under which seekers have always sat; Narada, the wandering singer who carries devotion between worlds; the gandharvas' master musician; Kapila, the philosopher counted born-perfect. Roots, song, and insight — the catalogue's range in a single verse.
verses.connection: The ashvattha planted here will return, inverted, as chapter 15's opening image.
verses.reflection: Notice the best tree, the best sage, the best song you meet today — vibhuti-spotting as a walk.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम् ।
ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम्॥
verses.transliteration:
uccaiḥśravasamaśvānāṃ viddhi māmamṛtodbhavam
airāvataṃ gajendrāṇāṃ narāṇāṃ ca narādhipam
verses.essence: Of horses know me as Uchchaihshravas, born of the nectar; of lordly elephants, Airavata; and among men, the king.
verses.translation: Know Me as Ucchaisravas born of nectar, among horses; among lordly elephants (I am) the Airavata; and, among men, the king.

anvaya.meaning: of horses, Uchchaihshravas
know me — born of the nectar(-churning)
of lordly elephants, Airavata
and of men, the ruler of men

verses.explanation: Two beasts that rose from the churned ocean of immortality — the white horse of heaven, Indra's own elephant — and then, among humans, simply: the king. Wherever sovereignty genuinely sits, the verse says, something of his sits in it; authority at its truest is borrowed radiance.
verses.connection: Royalty in animal and human form: the splendor of rule traced to its lender.
verses.reflection: Even among animals and instruments, the excellence is His — honor one excellent creature today.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक् ।
प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः॥
verses.transliteration:
āyudhānāmahaṃ vajraṃ dhenūnāmasmi kāmadhuk
prajanaścāsmi kandarpaḥ sarpāṇāmasmi vāsukiḥ
verses.essence: Of weapons I am the thunderbolt; of cows, the wish-fulfilling Kamadhuk; as progenitor I am Kandarpa, the god of love; of serpents I am Vasuki.
verses.translation: Among weapons I am the thunderbolt; among cows I am the wish-fulfilling cow called Kamadhenu; I am the progenitor, the god of love; among serpents I am Vasuki.

anvaya.meaning: of weapons I am the vajra
of milch-cows, the Kamadhuk
and as the begetter I am Kandarpa
of serpents I am Vasuki

verses.explanation: The thunderbolt forged from a sage's bones; the cow that milks whatever is wished; and — startlingly — Kandarpa, desire itself in its generative office, the love that continues the world. Eros, in its dharmic channel (7.11's promise kept), stands in the divine inventory beside the holiest relics.
verses.connection: Echoes 7.11: rightful desire as divine presence, here named as the love-god himself.
verses.reflection: Of weapons, desires, serpents — even your dangerous capacities have a consecrated best. Find yours.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम् ।
पितॄणामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
anantaścāsmi nāgānāṃ varuṇo yādasāmaham
pitṝṇāmaryamā cāsmi yamaḥ saṃyamatāmaham
verses.essence: Of the nagas I am Ananta; of water-beings I am Varuna; of the ancestors I am Aryama; of the subduers I am Yama.
verses.translation: I am Ananta among the Nagas; I am Varuna among water-deities; Aryaman among the Manes I am; I am Yama among the governors.

anvaya.meaning: and of the nagas I am Ananta
of water-beings I am Varuna
of the ancestors I am Aryama
of restrainers I am Yama

verses.explanation: Ananta, the endless serpent on whom Vishnu rests; the old sea-sovereign; the chief of the fathers — and Yama, death-as-judge, named without flinching among the glories. Even the power that ends lives is, rightly seen, his order-keeping face (9.19 already said as much).
verses.connection: The catalogue's shadow-entries: restraint and death claimed as calmly as light.
verses.reflection: Even among the binders and the dead, He is chief — fear one fewer category today.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम् ।
मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम्॥
verses.transliteration:
prahlādaścāsmi daityānāṃ kālaḥ kalayatāmaham
mṛgāṇāṃ ca mṛgendro'haṃ vainateyaśca pakṣiṇām
verses.essence: Of the daityas I am Prahlada; of reckoners I am Time; of beasts I am the lion; of birds, Garuda, son of Vinata.
verses.translation: And, I am Prahlada among the demons, among the reckoners I am time; among beasts I am the lion, and Vainateya (Garuda) among birds.

anvaya.meaning: and of the daityas I am Prahlada
of reckoners I am Time
of beasts, the lord of beasts (lion)
and of birds, the son of Vinata (Garuda)

verses.explanation: The most moving entry in the list: among the demons themselves, Prahlada — the daitya child whose devotion no torture could extinguish. Even in the lineage defined by opposition to God, God claims his own. No camp, the verse implies, is beyond having a saint.
verses.connection: Grace inside the enemy line: the catalogue refuses to concede any territory entirely.
verses.reflection: Among the devious and the counters of time, His glory still indexes — nothing is outside the catalog.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम् ।
झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी॥
verses.transliteration:
pavanaḥ pavatāmasmi rāmaḥ śastrabhṛtāmaham
jhaṣāṇāṃ makaraścāsmi srotasāmasmi jāhnavī
verses.essence: Of purifiers I am the wind; of weapon-bearers I am Rama; of fishes I am the makara; of rivers I am the Ganga.
verses.translation: Among the purifiers (or the speeders) I am the wind; Rama among the warriors am I; among the fishes I am the shark; among the streams I am the Ganga.

anvaya.meaning: of purifiers I am the wind
of weapon-bearers I am Rama
and of fishes I am the makara
of streams I am the Jahnavi (Ganga)

verses.explanation: Rama — the other great avatara, dharma with a bow — named by Krishna as himself: one descent saluting another across the ages. Beside him, the wind that cleanses by passing through, and the river that has carried more prayers than any water on earth.
verses.connection: Avatara recognizing avatara: the catalogue's quiet self-reference.
verses.reflection: Be the Ganga among your own rivers today: the version of your work that purifies as it flows.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन ।
अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
sargāṇāmādirantaśca madhyaṃ caivāhamarjuna
adhyātmavidyā vidyānāṃ vādaḥ pravadatāmaham
verses.essence: Of creations I am the beginning, the end, and also the middle, Arjuna; of sciences, the science of the Self; of those who debate, I am the reasoned discourse.
verses.translation: Among creations I am the beginning, the middle and also the end, O Arjuna; among the sciences I am the science of the Self; and I am the logic among controversialists.

anvaya.meaning: of creations, the beginning and end
and the middle indeed, I, O Arjuna
of sciences, the science of the Self
of disputants' speech, I am vada

verses.explanation: Among all branches of knowledge, the one that studies the knower; and among all modes of argument, vāda — debate conducted to find truth rather than to win (the tradition distinguished it sharply from mere wrangling). Even disagreement, done honestly, is a place he lives.
verses.connection: Knowledge and honest argument enrolled: the intellect's best uses as vibhuti.
verses.reflection: Of all your studies, return to the science of the Self today — vidyas crowned by adhyatma-vidya.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च ।
अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताहं विश्वतोमुखः॥
verses.transliteration:
akṣarāṇāmakāro'smi dvandvaḥ sāmāsikasya ca
ahamevākṣayaḥ kālo dhātāhaṃ viśvatomukhaḥ
verses.essence: Of letters I am the letter A; of compounds, the dvandva; I alone am inexhaustible Time; I am the Ordainer facing every direction.
verses.translation: Among the letters of the alphabets, the letter 'A' I am and the dual among the compounds. I am verily the inexhaustible or everlasting time; I am the dispenser (of the fruits of actions) having faces in all directions.

anvaya.meaning: of letters I am the A
and of compounds, the dvandva
I alone am imperishable Time
I am the Dispenser, facing all ways

verses.explanation: The first vowel, latent in every consonant — speech's hidden ground; and among grammatical compounds, the dvandva, where both members keep equal weight: even grammar's most egalitarian form is noted. Then the scale leaps: akṣayaḥ kālaḥ, Time that does not run out — the second naming of Time, now as endurance rather than reckoning.
verses.connection: From the smallest unit of language to duration itself — the catalogue's widest single span.
verses.reflection: Watch Time today as undecaying — and the Dispenser facing every direction, including yours.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम् ।
कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा॥
verses.transliteration:
mṛtyuḥ sarvaharaścāhamudbhavaśca bhaviṣyatām
kīrtiḥ śrīrvākca nārīṇāṃ smṛtirmedhā dhṛtiḥ kṣamā
verses.essence: I am all-seizing death, and the origin of all that is to come; among feminine powers: fame, fortune, speech, memory, intelligence, constancy and patience.
verses.translation: And I am the all-devouring Death, and the prosperity of those who are to be prosperous; among the feminine alities (I am) fame, prosperity, speech, memory, intelligence, firmness and forgiveness.

anvaya.meaning: I am death, the all-seizing
and the origin of things to come
of the feminine: fame, fortune, speech
memory, intelligence, constancy, patience

verses.explanation: The bluntest line in the catalogue — mṛtyuḥ sarva-haraḥ, the death that takes everything — set against udbhava, the springing-up of all futures: one hand closes, the other opens, same owner. Then seven goddesses of the inner life, the powers a person most hopes to keep: all of them named as her, as him.
verses.connection: Death and the future in one breath — the catalogue's refusal to look away, continued from 10.29.
verses.reflection: Hold both honestly: He is death the seizer and the seven graces — fame, fortune, speech, memory among them.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम् ।
मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकरः॥
verses.transliteration:
bṛhatsāma tathā sāmnāṃ gāyatrī chandasāmaham
māsānāṃ mārgaśīrṣo'hamṛtūnāṃ kusumākaraḥ
verses.essence: Of hymns I am the Brihat-saman; of meters I am the Gayatri; of months, Margashirsha; of seasons, the flower-bearing spring.
verses.translation: Among the hymns also I am the Brihatsaman; among metres Gayatri am I; among the montsh I am the Margasirsha; among the seasons (I am) the flowery season.

anvaya.meaning: likewise of samans, the Brihat-saman
of meters I am the Gayatri
of months I am Margashirsha
of seasons, the flower-maker (spring)

verses.explanation: The Gayatri — the verse recited at more dawns than any other words humans have spoken; the harvest month when the old year's work is gathered; and kusumākara, spring, the season that makes flowers. The calendar itself has summits, and he claims them.
verses.connection: Time's gentler faces after 10.34's severe ones: the year's song, the year's bloom.
verses.reflection: Of your months and seasons, find the flowering one — and sing one Brihat-sama line of your own.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ।
जयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
dyūtaṃ chalayatāmasmi tejastejasvināmaham
jayo'smi vyavasāyo'smi sattvaṃ sattvavatāmaham
verses.essence: I am the gambling of the cunning; I am the splendor of the splendid; I am victory, I am resolve; I am the goodness of the good.
verses.translation: I am the gambling of the fraudulent; I am the splendour of the splendid; I am victory; I am determination (of those who are determined); I am the goodness of the good.

anvaya.meaning: I am the dicing of the deceitful
the splendor of the splendid
I am victory, I am determined effort
the sattva of the sattva-endowed

verses.explanation: The list's most unnerving entry: dyūtam, the dice — the very game that gambled away Arjuna's kingdom and dignity. Even the world's sharp practice draws its cleverness from the one source; nothing, not even what wounded the listener most, runs on independent power. Honesty this complete is the catalogue's deepest claim.
verses.connection: The hardest vibhuti for Arjuna to hear — and proof the list excludes nothing.
verses.reflection: Even the gambler's dice are claimed — let no corner of your day pretend to be outside Him.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनञ्जयः ।
मुनीनामप्यहं व्यासः कवीनामुशना कविः॥
verses.transliteration:
vṛṣṇīnāṃ vāsudevo'smi pāṇḍavānāṃ dhanañjayaḥ
munīnāmapyahaṃ vyāsaḥ kavīnāmuśanā kaviḥ
verses.essence: Of the Vrishnis I am Vasudeva; of the Pandavas, Dhananjaya; of the sages I am Vyasa; of the seers, the poet Ushana.
verses.translation: Among the Vrishnis I am Vaasudeva; among the Pandavas I am Arjuna; among the sages I am Vyasa; among the Poets I am Usanas, the poet.

anvaya.meaning: of the Vrishnis I am Vasudeva
of the Pandavas, Dhananjaya (Arjuna)
of the munis too I am Vyasa
of the kavis, the poet Ushana

verses.explanation: The catalogue turns and looks at its own audience: among the Pandavas — *you*, Arjuna. The listener discovers himself inside the list of glories he has been hearing; his own excellence was never his private property. And Vyasa: the teller of this very tale, claimed by his own character.
verses.connection: The list's most intimate move: the hearer, and the author, enrolled among the vibhutis.
verses.reflection: Of the Pandavas, Dhananjaya: hear yourself included in the list, and stand a little straighter.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम् ।
मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
daṇḍo damayatāmasmi nītirasmi jigīṣatām
maunaṃ caivāsmi guhyānāṃ jñānaṃ jñānavatāmaham
verses.essence: Of punishers I am the rod; of those who seek victory I am statecraft; of secrets I am silence; of the wise I am wisdom.
verses.translation: Of those who punish, I am the sceptre; among those who seek victory, I am statesmanship; and also among secrets, I am silence; knowledge among knowers I am.

anvaya.meaning: of those who restrain, I am the rod
of those seeking victory, I am statecraft
and of secrets I am silence
of the knowing, I am knowledge itself

verses.explanation: The catalogue's last formula-verse closes a spiral: of all secrets, mauna — silence, the secret that keeps itself — and of all knowers, the knowing in them. The list of words about God ends by bowing to the wordless and to the very light by which any list is read.
verses.connection: The final entries point past the catalogue: silence and knowledge — where enumeration retires.
verses.reflection: Of restraints, the rod; of secrets, silence — practice one noble silence today and call it His.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन ।
न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम्॥
verses.transliteration:
yaccāpi sarvabhūtānāṃ bījaṃ tadahamarjuna
na tadasti vinā yatsyānmayā bhūtaṃ carācaram
verses.essence: And whatever is the seed of all beings, Arjuna, that am I. There is no being, moving or unmoving, that could exist without me.
verses.translation: And whatever is the seed of all beings, that also am I, O Arjuna; there is no being, whether moving or unmoving, that can exist without Me.

anvaya.meaning: and whatever, of all beings
is the seed — that am I, Arjuna

anvaya.word: न तत् अस्ति विना यत् स्यात्
anvaya.meaning: there is nothing that could be without
me — being moving or unmoving

verses.explanation: The closing clause that makes the whole list redundant: bījam — the seed of everything, listed or not. Na tad asti vinā yat syān mayā: nothing exists that could exist without me. The seventy-odd entries were never an inventory of where he is; they were training wheels for seeing that there is no 'where he isn't.'
verses.connection: Dissolves the catalogue into its principle: the highlights were samples of a total presence.
verses.reflection: Test the closing claim on anything you meet: no being, moving or unmoving, exists without Him.
verses.background: Krishna consents — 'hanta, I will tell you My chief glories, for there is no end to My extent' — and the great catalogue unrolls over the silent battlefield: the Self in every heart first, then sun among lights, OM among words, Himalaya among the unmoving, lion among beasts, Ganga among rivers, Arjuna among Pandavas, down to the dice of gamblers and silence among secrets.

verses.devanagari:
नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परन्तप ।
एष तूद्देशतः प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरो मया॥
verses.transliteration:
nānto'sti mama divyānāṃ vibhūtīnāṃ parantapa
eṣa tūddeśataḥ prokto vibhūtervistaro mayā
verses.essence: There is no end to my divine manifestations, Parantapa. What I have declared is but an indication of my infinite glory.
verses.translation: There is no end to My divine gloreis, O Arjuna, but this is a brief statement by Me of the particulars of My divine glories.

anvaya.meaning: there is no end of my divine
manifestations, O Parantapa
but this has been spoken by way of indication
the extent of the vibhuti, by me

verses.explanation: The honest accounting promised in 10.19 is delivered: uddeśataḥ — by way of pointing, a finger and not the moon. Every catalogue of the infinite is a brochure; the verse files it as such, lest anyone mistake seventy windows for the whole sky.
verses.connection: Bookends 10.19: the sampler closed with its own disclaimer.
verses.reflection: Stop counting today — the glories have no end; the catalog was only ever a training of the eye.
verses.background: The catalogue closes itself: there is no end to My divine manifestations; this was a mere indication. Whatever is glorious, prosperous, or powerful anywhere — know it sprung from a spark of My splendor. But what need of all this detail? I stand sustaining this entire universe with a single fragment of Myself.

verses.devanagari:
यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा ।
तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसम्भवम्॥
verses.transliteration:
yadyadvibhūtimatsattvaṃ śrīmadūrjitameva vā
tattadevāvagaccha tvaṃ mama tejoṃśasambhavam
verses.essence: Whatever being is glorious, prosperous or powerful — understand that to have sprung from but a spark of my splendor.
verses.translation: Whatever being there is glorious, prosperous or powerful, that know thou to be a manifestation of a part of My splendour.

anvaya.meaning: whatever being possesses glory
or prosperity, or might
that, that — understand
as born of a fragment of my splendor

verses.explanation: The portable version of the entire chapter, issued for use after the list is forgotten: wherever you meet excellence — any beauty, any force, any flourishing — read it as tejo-'ṁśa, a spark off the one fire. A rule of thumb that turns the whole world into the catalogue's next page. Yad yad vibhutimat sattvam — whatever being carries glory, grace, or power, know it sprung from a fragment of My splendor: the master key handed over at the catalog's end. Commentators call this verse the retirement of the list: no inventory needed once the rule is held. Mama tejo-'msha-sambhavam — every excellence anywhere, one spark's worth of a single fire.
verses.connection: The take-home formula: 10.21–38 compressed into one habit of perception.
verses.reflection: Use the master key all day: wherever you see splendor or strength, read one spark of His fire.
verses.background: The catalogue closes itself: there is no end to My divine manifestations; this was a mere indication. Whatever is glorious, prosperous, or powerful anywhere — know it sprung from a spark of My splendor. But what need of all this detail? I stand sustaining this entire universe with a single fragment of Myself.

verses.devanagari:
अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन ।
विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत्॥
verses.transliteration:
athavā bahunaitena kiṃ jñātena tavārjuna
viṣṭabhyāhamidaṃ kṛtsnamekāṃśena sthito jagat
verses.essence: But what need have you of all this detailed knowledge, Arjuna? I stand, supporting this entire universe with a single fragment of myself.
verses.translation: But, of what avail to thee is the knowledge of all these details, O Arjuna? I exist, supporting this whole world by one part of Myself.

anvaya.meaning: or rather, with this much (detail)
what (use) of it known, to you, Arjuna
propping up, I, this entire
universe — by one fragment — stand

verses.explanation: The chapter ends by waving away its own magnificence: atha vā — but really, why all this? Then the sentence that out-scales everything before it: ekāṁśena, with one fragment I hold the whole. All the worlds, all the catalogue, all the glory — a sliver of him; the rest exceeds existence itself. Arjuna's next request, to *see* this, becomes inevitable.
verses.connection: The closing escalation that detonates into chapter 11: hearing is finished; seeing must begin.
verses.reflection: Carry the final compression with you: one fragment of Him sustains all this. Walk inside that fragment.
verses.background: The catalogue closes itself: there is no end to My divine manifestations; this was a mere indication. Whatever is glorious, prosperous, or powerful anywhere — know it sprung from a spark of My splendor. But what need of all this detail? I stand sustaining this entire universe with a single fragment of Myself.
